title: Thapsus schraderi
taxonomy: Thapsus schraderi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Thapsus. Within the Rubiaceae family, Thapsus schraderi is part of the group of plants commonly known as coffee of the mountain or mountain coffee. The species is native to tropical Africa. Further classification may vary depending on the source and taxonomic system used.
uses: Thapsus schraderi, also known as shrubby Thapsia, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Some studies suggest that it may have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine, particularly in North African and Middle Eastern cultures. Thapsus schraderi is said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. However, its medicinal uses should be approached with caution, as some sources indicate that it may be toxic in large quantities.
